title: A simple tasting path
summary: Use four checks to compare styles.

signals.title: Look
signals.body: Color and clarity narrow the likely style.

signals.title: Smell
signals.body: Yeast, hops, smoke, or malt leads.

signals.title: Taste
signals.body: Check sweetness, bitterness, body, and carbonation.

signals.title: Finish
signals.body: Notice if it ends crisp, dry, or warm.

guardrails.title: Use plain words
guardrails.body: Avoid tasting notes nobody can picture.

guardrails.title: Compare gently
guardrails.body: One contrast per beer is enough.

guardrails.title: Pair last
guardrails.body: Food makes more sense after the style cue.
